result:
Standard policy: refund to the original tender, proportionally. If the order used $30 gift card + $70 card, a $50 refund returns $15 to the gift card (restore balance, extending expiry or not is a separate call) and $35 to the card. Simpler alternative is "card first, gift card last" — refund up to the card charge before touching the gift card — which favors the customer but complicates accounting because the refunded tender mix no longer matches the original.

Code path: load the original payment allocation from the order (you'll need to persist it at checkout — gift_card_amount, card_amount, gift_card_id), compute the refund split, then in one transaction credit the gift card balance (with the same row lock as consumption) and issue the card refund via your PSP. If the PSP call fails after the gift card credit, you need a compensating debit or a retry queue — same conditional-debit pattern as the original charge, inverted.

Watch for: refunds after gift card expiry (credit to a new card? refuse? policy call), and refunds that exceed the original gift card portion (cap the gift card credit at what was consumed, rest goes to card).
